channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: Caja de Ahorros de Panamá apuesta por ecosistemas y alianzas para acelerar su digitalización inclusiva
item.description: Caja de Ahorros de Panamá estrenó un área dedicada a alianzas estratégicas y ecosistemas para potenciar sus desarrollos digitales –como una versión mejorada de su banca móvil– y articular una propuesta de valor más amplia, orientada a consolidarse como el banco principal de sus clientes, a través de experiencias cotidianas y conectadas. “La aspiración es poder estar en la cotidianidad de nuestros clientes y desarrollarles un ecosistema que al final seamos nosotros su banco principal”, dijo a iupana Aldo Ríos, director de innovación y transformación del banco estatal panameño. Accede al análisis que impulsa la toma de decisiones en la banca latinoamericana Este contenido exclusivo está disponible solo para miembros registrados de iupana.

item.title: Is Commercial Auto Having It’s ‘Sprinkler Moment’?
item.description: The commercial auto insurance industry may be approaching a tipping point, and dashcams are at the center of it. Telematics-based safety programs are increasingly embedded in commercial auto insurance offerings, using a range of go-to-market strategies from premium subsidization to mandatory participation. Among the different telematics-based solutions, dashcams are emerging as one of the key tools for building an effective connected commercial auto program.

item.title: Arctic Sea Ice Hits Lowest Winter Level as Unprecedented Heat Smashes Global Records
item.description: Vital Arctic sea ice shrank to tie its lowest measured level for the winter, the season when ice grows, as a warming Earth shattered records across the continents. The shrinking Arctic sea ice was announced Thursday [March 26] as temperatures broke March heat records across the United States, all over Mexico, in Australia, across Northern Africa and through parts of Northern Europe. Each year Arctic sea ice grows over the cold winter and shrinks in the heat of the summer.

item.title: Howden appoints Mani Tsang as head of claims in Hong Kong
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Record US$785bn of reinsurance capital drives APAC market softening during 1.4 renewals: Aon
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Asian renewals see broad softening, but outcomes diverge by territory and loss experience
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Q1 2026 Shatters Venture Funding Records As AI Boom Pushes Startup Investment To Nearly $300B
item.description: In fact, startup investment in the first quarter of 2026 alone totaled close to 70% of all venture capital spending in 2025 . The first quarter of 2026 was unlike any other for venture investment, driven by unprecedented spending on AI compute and frontier labs. Four of the five largest venture rounds ever recorded were closed in Q1 2026, with frontier labs OpenAI ($120 billion), Anthropic ($30 billion), xAI ($20 billion) and self-driving company Waymo ($16 billion) collectively raising $186 billion, or 64% of global venture investment in the quarter.

item.title: Alternative / ILS reinsurance capital grew 18% to $136bn in 2025: Aon
item.description: Alternative or third-party reinsurance capital, so that deployed through insurance-linked securities and related collateralized structures, grew by 18% over the course of 2025 and 10% just in the fourth-quarter to reach a record $136 billion at the end of the year, according to the latest estimate from broker Aon. Overall reinsurance capital grew by around 10% in calendar year 2025, according to Aon’s estimate, with alternative and ILS capital from third-party investors the major driver. Having previously reported that total reinsurance capital reached $760 billion by the end of September 2025, Aon now puts the total 3% higher at $785 billion by the end of December.

item.title: CatIQ lowers Ontario and Quebec ice storm loss estimate to C$466m
item.description: CatIQ, Canada’s insured loss and exposure index provider, has issued its fifth and final industry loss estimate from the ice storm that struck Ontario and Quebec in late March 2025, putting the insured market loss at CAD $466 million, a 5% decrease from its fourth loss estimate of CAD $490 million that was issued in September. We covered CatIQ’s first loss estimate from the ice storm, which was issued on April 30, where losses had surpassed CAD $340 million. CatIQ’s loss estimate covers property (both commercial and residential) and vehicle (motor) claims including additional loss adjustment expenses.

item.title: Reinsurance demand up ~10% at April 1st renewals, significant rate reductions seen: Aon
item.description: insurers also used favorable buyer conditions to transfer more risk to reinsurers through increased limits, frequency covers and proportional transactions.” The broker’s report also highlighted that global reinsurance capital reached a record $785 billion at April 1st, which according to the firm, enabled significant risk-adjusted rate reductions for its insurer clients at the renewal, extending the favourable momentum seen during the January 1 reinsurance renewal period. Global demand for reinsurance increased by approximately 10% at the April 1st reinsurance renewals, as buyers capitalised on favourable market conditions to secure more comprehensive protection, with some expected to return to the market post-renewal to explore additional purchases, according to broker Aon’s April renewal report. “In key Asia Pacific markets – including Japan, Korea and India – reinsurance buyers secured double‑digit rate reductions, supported by plentiful capacity and a period of relatively benign catastrophe losses,” Aon explained.

item.title: Geneva Association flags persistent gaps in cyber hygiene and insurance adoption
item.description: A recent Geneva Association report warns that as cyber incidents grow more frequent, sophisticated, and costly, many firms still have persistent gaps in cyber hygiene and risk management, underscoring the urgent need to strengthen resilience, with cyber insurance playing a critical role. Yet many incidents still stem from basic, preventable vulnerabilities—such as phishing attacks, weak passwords, unpatched software, and misconfigured systems—highlighting persistent gaps in cyber hygiene and risk management. The Geneva Association described cyber insurance as a potentially powerful, though still under-realised, governance mechanism—one that can positively shape firm behaviour, incentivise risk prevention and mitigation, and provide critical expertise and financial support when incidents occur.

item.title: Swiss Re names Henock Teklu as its new CTO and Chief of Staff
item.description: Swiss Re has appointed Henock Teklu as its new Group Chief Transformation Officer and Group Chief of Staff. With 20 years of leadership experience across investment banking, insurance, and asset management, Teklu brings extensive strategic expertise to Swiss Re. Teklu joins the firm from BlackRock Investment Management in New York, where he spent over eight years, most recently serving as a Managing Director.

item.title: Coface appoints Carmen Chow as Head of Commercial for Hong Kong
item.description: Coface, a provider of credit insurance and risk management solutions, has announced the appointment of Carmen Chow as Head of Commercial for Hong Kong, effective 1st April 2026. In her new role, Chow will be responsible for strengthening Coface’s commercial performance strategy and deepening client engagement in the Hong Kong market. Chris Murphy, Chief Executive and General Manager (Hong Kong), Coface, commented, “We are delighted to have Carmen lead our commercial team in Hong Kong.

item.title: Bank of England Says Iran War Has Boosted Threats to Financial Stability
item.description: The Bank of England said on Wednesday that the Iran war had dealt “a substantial negative supply shock” to the world economy, increasing the danger that pre-existing threats to financial stability materialize. This increases the possibility of large, frequent and potentially overlapping shocks and periods of intense volatility,” the BoE’s Financial Policy Committee said in a quarterly update. Iran Conflict Pushes Up Inflation, Financing Costs The U.S.

item.title: Viewpoint: How Will the Middle East War Affect the Insurance Sector?
item.description: The recent escalation of the Middle East war introduces geopolitical instability with potential long-lasting effects on commodities, financing conditions, global supply chains, and macro-credit conditions depending on the duration and scale of the conflict. S&P Global Ratings’ base case for the Middle East war assumes that elevated hostilities will persist into early April, with the Strait of Hormuz facing material disruptions. (Editor’s note: S&P Global Ratings says there is a high degree of unpredictability around the duration and scale of the Middle East war and its potential effect on commodity prices, supply chains, economies, and credit conditions.

item.title: Vermont Defends Its Climate Superfund Law Against Trump Administration Lawsuit
item.description: Vermont became the first state to enact a climate superfund law, modeled on the federal superfund law that taxed petroleum and chemical companies to pay to clean up sites polluted by toxic waste. The Department of Justice also sued Vermont and New York after President Donald Trump ordered Attorney General Pam Bondi to take action against states that may be overreaching their authority in how they regulate energy development. Vermont’s effort to make fossil fuel companies pay for damage caused by climate change was tested Monday in a federal courtroom, where the state argued that two lawsuits challenging its groundbreaking 2024 law should be thrown out.

item.title: Gen Re strengthens leadership with Nancy Roos appointment to Executive Board
item.description: Gen Re has appointed Nancy Roos to the Executive Board of General Reinsurance AG, effective April 1, 2026, reinforcing its leadership team and governance capabilities. Charlie Shamieh, Chairman of Gen Re and Chair of the Executive Board of General Reinsurance AG, highlighted Roos’ impact, noting her leadership of the international finance function and her ability to guide the organisation through complex regulatory environments. Roos, who will continue in her role as Chief Financial Officer, steps into the position with more than two decades of experience at Gen Re.

item.title: Talanx AG strengthens capital position with €1bn dual bond issuance
item.description: Talanx AG has successfully raised €1 billion through a dual bond issuance, reinforcing its capital structure and supporting long-term financial flexibility. The issuance has been assigned a strong ‘AA-’ rating by S&P Global Ratings and will be listed on the Luxembourg Stock Exchange, underscoring investor confidence in Talanx’s financial strength and strategy. This move highlights Talanx’s proactive approach to capital management, positioning the group to maintain financial resilience while continuing to invest in growth and innovation across its global insurance operations.

item.title: duPont REGISTRY Group acquires LLP Exotic Auto Finance
item.description: duPont REGISTRY Group has acquired LLP Exotic Auto Finance , expanding its capabilities in leasing and lending for high-end vehicles. The deal adds a dedicated finance layer to duPont REGISTRY’s ecosystem, which already spans marketplace, media, events, and auctions, and is expected to improve conversion by enabling customers to move from discovery to financing within a single platform. The move further positions duPont REGISTRY as a vertically integrated player across the luxury automotive lifecycle, from listing and discovery to ownership and financing.

item.title: Farmers targets 1,700 new agents with elite push
item.description: The Elite program targets candidates with at least $500,000 in available capital and offers tiered benefits, including enhanced support, priority service, and additional marketing incentives. The move reflects a push toward an “entrepreneurial agency model,” where owners operate independently while leveraging Farmers’ brand and infrastructure. Farmers is aiming to appoint 1,700 new agency owners in 2026 as part of a broader effort to reignite organic growth and expand its distribution footprint.

item.title: BBVA Seguros crece un 63% en primas de Vida en 2025
item.description: BBVA Seguros cerró 2025 con un espectacular incremento del 62,5% en su volumen total de primas de Vida, que llegó a 2.163 millones de euros. También destacan los comportamientos del Plan de Ahorro 5 (SIALP), que crece un 109,7% hasta los 23,7 millones, y del Plan de Previsión Asegurado (PPA), que suma 72,9 millones en primas tras aumentar un 25,4%. La razón fundamental se sitúa en el segmento de Vida Ahorro Individual, que alcanzó 1.574,3 millones, con un crecimiento del 124%.

item.title: PIB Group Iberia gestionó 500 millones de euros en 2025
item.description: PIB Group Iberia ha cerrado el ejercicio 2025 con 500 millones de euros en primas gestionadas. «El proyecto de PIB Group en Iberia sigue avanzando con una visión a largo plazo, en la que combinamos crecimiento, especialización y cercanía al cliente. Seguimos manteniéndonos como un actor de M&A relevante en el mercado asegurador de iberia, priorizando Portugal», hace balance Martín Navaz, presidente y CEO de PIB Group Iberia.

item.title: Las tormentas convectivas severas suponen en 2025 casi la mitad de las pérdidas aseguradas por catástrofes
item.description: Las tormentas convectivas severas (SCS) se han consolidado como una de las principales causas de pérdidas aseguradas a nivel mundial, según revela un nuevo informe de Allianz Commercial . De acuerdo con el análisis, las SCS representaron casi la mitad de las pérdidas aseguradas por catástrofes naturales registradas el año pasad o, con un volumen superior a 60.000 millones de dólares. Dentro de este conjunto, el granizo se identifica como el principal factor de daño, concentrando entre el 50% y el 80% de las pérdidas, debido a su impacto directo sobre edificios, vehículos, infraestructuras industriales y activos de alto valor como plantas de energía renovable o aeronaves.

item.title: Analyst Predicts Insurance AI Investments Will End in Failure
item.description: (Image credit: Minnesota Historical Society.) A self-described industry analyst is warning insurers that their investments in artificial intelligence may be introducing new forms of risk into an already highly regulated business—and predicts those risks will ultimately outweigh the benefits. “Insurance decisions must be explainable, auditable, and defensible,” says Dr. Alistair Prewitt-Crane, an independent analyst.

item.title: EPA Watchdog: Nation’s Most Contaminated Sites Vulnerable to Floods, Wildfires
item.description: Some 100 of the nation’s most contaminated toxic waste sites are in areas prone to flooding and wildfires, a potential public health threat to millions of Americans in surrounding communities, the internal watchdog at the U.S. But the new reports issued by the IG’s remaining staff still lay out the risks posed by a warming planet to the nation’s most dangerous toxic waste sites. The review also found 31 sites in areas at high risk for wildfires.

item.title: North Korea-Linked Hack Hit Axios Software That Powers Online Services
item.description: Hackers linked to North Korea breached behind-the-scenes software that runs many common online functions in an effort to steal login information that could enable further cyber operations, Google said on Tuesday. The hackers targeted Axios, a program that connects apps and web services, by adding their own malicious software to an update issued Monday, Google and independent cyber researchers said after the hack came to light early on Tuesday. Related: Axios Software Tool Used by Millions Compromised in Hack “Every time you load a website, check your bank balance, or open an app on your phone, there’s a good chance Axios is running somewhere in the background making that work,” said Tom Hegel, a senior researcher at SentinelOne.

item.title: Stellantis Recalling 178,000 Mini Vans in US Over Side-Curtain Airbag Issue
item.description: Stellantis will recall 178,246 mini vans in the U.S. over improperly sealed side curtain airbags, the U.S. National Highway Traffic Safety Administration said on Tuesday.

item.title: MLX demerger from Market Lane makes ‘significant headway’
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Aon hands Nick Chapman expanded marine risk engineering lead role in Asia
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Howden hires Axa’s Andy Buddell as Asia COO
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Specialty MGA appoints Uwe Sievers to drive Japan growth strategy
item.description: Specialty MGA, part of MNK Group, has appointed Uwe Sievers as Director of Business Development, Japan, tasking him with leading the expansion of its focused offering in the Japanese market. Over that period, Sievers played a pivotal role in advancing multiple lines of business, shaping long-term strategy, and driving sustained growth across the Japanese operation. Japan is an incredibly important strategic market for us and Uwe’s appointment strengthens our ability to support Japanese clients and partners in a more focused and forward‑looking way.” Sievers added: “I am very pleased to be joining Specialty MGA at a time when the Japanese market is opening up to new forms of capacity.

item.title: Fitch upgrades Athora’s IFS ratings to A+ following PIC acquisition
item.description: The rating upgrades follow Athora’s completion of its acquisition of Pension Insurance Corporation Group Limited , including its wholly owned subsidiary, Pension Insurance Corporation plc (PIC), announced on 27th March 2026. Fitch believes the acquisition strengthens Athora’s asset risk profile, enhances Athora’s financial performance, and leads to larger, more diversified operations. Fitch Ratings has upgraded the Insurer Financial Strength Ratings of SRLEV N.V.

item.title: Nancy Roos joins the Executive Board of General Reinsurance AG
item.description: Gen Re, the global reinsurance subsidiary of Berkshire Hathaway, has appointed Nancy Roos to the Executive Board of General Reinsurance AG, effective April 1st, 2026, following the retirement of Mike O’Dea. Charlie Shamieh, Gen Re’s Chairman and Chair of General Reinsurance AG’s Executive Board, commented, “Since assuming the role of CFO of General Reinsurance AG, Nancy has led the international finance function through an increasingly complex reporting and regulatory environment. I am confident that her deep institutional knowledge, strategic insight, and proven leadership will ideally complement the Executive Board.” He continued, “On the occasion of his retirement, I’d also like to extend my deep appreciation to Mike O’Dea for his remarkable contributions over the course of his career, as our global Gen Re Group CFO and longstanding member of the General Reinsurance AG’s Executive Board.

item.title: US IPO Prospects in Limbo as War Dents Optimism Ahead of SpaceX
item.description: For banks, there’s a ray of hope — that SpaceX’s potential blockbuster listing could still go ahead, and make their whole year. The companies that typically make up the backbone of the US initial public offering market are eyeing the Iran war for signs volatility could boil over and wreck their plans. After a first quarter marked by stop-and-start activity for IPOs, talk has turned to the implications of the raging conflict and the lingering effect of artificial intelligence scares.

item.title: NAIC Issues Nationwide Data Call to Homeowners Insurers
item.description: The National Association of Insurance Commissioners has issued a nationwide data call to insurers in order to get a better understand of the U.S. It will also help us ensure companies have the capital they need to quickly and fully pay claims in some of consumers’ most vulnerable moments,” said Florida Insurance Commissioner Mike Yaworsky, who is also the chair of NAIC’s Homeowners Market Data Call Task Force. The data, intended to be collected at the ZIP code level, will help regulators assess how coverage options and deductibles affect costs and access, evaluate mitigation efforts, monitor insurers’ financial strength, and better understand consumer awareness of insurance options, the organization said.

item.title: Dei Primus Holdings Launches LUCY, a Fully Autonomous Insurance Carrier
item.description: Dei Primus Holdings announced this week the formation of LUCY Insurance, a property/casualty carrier executives say is not an automation layer supporting human employees, but a fully autonomous operating system that performs the insurer’s primary business decisions. insurance carrier designed to operate without human decision-making in its core functions, replacing traditional underwriting, agent interaction and claims adjudication with a unified artificial intelligence platform. “We didn’t want to build tools for humans,” said Marcus Rawlings, CEO of Dei Primus and chair of the carrier’s governing board.

item.title: ‘One Liberty’: insurer completes APAC licence consolidation, simplifies branding
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: ‘A huge way to go in Asia’: region still land of opportunity, excitement, TMK Asia chief says
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Verisk Launches Rocket Repair for Claims Renovation Workflows
item.description: (Image source: Sprint Sanierung homepage.) Verisk (Jersey City, N.J.) has launched Rocket Repair , a digital platform designed to support renovation companies in Germany in managing property claims workflows and documentation. “With Rocket Repair, we’re bringing Verisk’s claims technology and data expertise to renovators who are critical to delivering on the promise to policyholders,” says Michael Rodenberg, Managing Director, Verisk Property GmbH. The platform enables renovators to capture and structure onsite data, centralize project information and improve collaboration across stakeholders involved in claims-related repair work.

item.title: War Risks Insurers Win Bid to Appeal UK Ruling on Jets Lost in Russia
item.description: Insurers including Chubb, Fidelis and Lloyd’s were granted permission on Tuesday to appeal against a London court ruling in favor of the world’s largest aircraft lessor AerCap over jets stuck in Russia since its war on Ukraine. London’s High Court ruled in June that AerCap can recover just over $1 billion from its “war risks insurers,” though that was less than the roughly $2 billion it sought under a broader all-risks clause. But Chubb, Fidelis and Lloyd’s were given permission to challenge the ruling at the Court of Appeal, with the appeal hearing expected to last up to five days.

item.title: AI, Geopolitical Volatility Lead to More Companies Missing Revenue Targets: Survey
item.description: A new survey of more than 1,000 global B2B executives finds that companies are falling short of revenue targets amid AI disruption and geopolitical uncertainty, according to new research from global consulting firm Bain & Company. Though companies remain ambitious, Bain’s 2026 B2B Growth Agenda report indicates that most lack the AI capabilities and clear value propositions needed to achieve their revenue goals. Firms expect 20% higher revenue growth rates in 2026 versus last year, with 91% confident they will meet their targets.

item.title: Whoop’s Wearable Fitness Tech Lands $575M From Athletes, Celebrities, Institutional Investors To Reach $10.1B Valuation
item.description: Whoop , which provides wearable fitness technology and a subscription platform that tracks physiological data for insights, announced Tuesday that it has raised $575 million in Series G funding at a $10.1 billion valuation. Collaborative Fund led its latest financing, which included participation from a slew of institutional investors, athletes and angels, including 2PointZero Group , Qatar Investment Authority , Mubadala Investment Co. The round marks a significant step-up in valuation from the $3.6 billion that Boston-based Whoop achieved in August 2021 when it raised a $200 million Series F round.

item.title: April 1 renewal saw Japan property cat rates return to early 2020s levels, says Howden Re
item.description: Risk-adjusted property-catastrophe rates-on-line in Japan reverted back to levels last seen in the early 2020s at the April 1 2026 reinsurance renewals, with catastrophe excess-of-loss programmes in the country witnessing risk-adjusted price reductions of up to 20%, according to Howden Re, the global reinsurance broker. At the Japan-focused April renewal season, Howden Re notes a continuation of the softening trend seen at January 1, in spite of the uncertain and volatile geopolitical landscape in the Middle East, which the broker says drove acute stress across multiple specialty lines around the world. Andy Souter, Head of Asia Pacific, Howden Re, said: “Japan rates are now broadly back to early twenties levels.

item.title: 5 Killed in Train-Van Collision in Mississippi
item.description: Five people were killed when a train and van collided in rural Mississippi, near Wiggins, authorities said. All five killed were in the van, Stone County Coroner Wayne Flurry said. No one aboard the train was reported injured.

item.title: Farmers Insurance Plans Historic, Rapid Expansion of Agency Force
item.description: Farmers Insurance has announced a plan to grow its agency force over the next year, and expects to appoint nearly 1,700 new agency owners—one of the largest single-year expansions in Farmers’ history. As part of the plan, Farmers is launching a new Elite Owner Program to attract high-net-worth agency owners with added support and financial incentives for rapid scaling. By onboarding 1,700 new agency owners — including an Elite tier of well-capitalized business leaders seeking to build or expand their portfolio — we’ll be injecting fresh energy into our distribution force.” Farmers said the Elite program will “significantly ramp up agency appointments across the country” by bringing in owners with at least $500,000 in capital.

item.title: FAA Imposes Restrictions on Some Landings at San Francisco Airport
item.description: The Federal Aviation Administration said on Tuesday that it was imposing new safety restrictions at San Francisco International Airport that will limit some landings and lead to significant delays. The FAA said the airport will experience delays due to a runway repaving project and the FAA decision to prohibit flights from making side-by-side approaches to San Francisco’s parallel east-west runways in clear weather. The FAA does not plan to lift the restrictions once the runway repaving operation is completed.

item.title: Admiral Business launches new taxi insurance
item.description: Admiral Business, the commercial insurer covering SMEs, sole traders and contractors, has launched a new taxi insurance proposition for public and private hire drivers across the UK. This marks the first time the business has entered the taxi market. The product provides single vehicle taxi cover, with plans to introduce taxi fleet insurance later this year.

item.title: Otto Quotes files ‘Insurance Zoo’ trademark
item.description: Otto Quotes, LLC has filed a trademark application for “Insurance Zoo.” The application, filed on March 27, 2026, covers both marketing services and insurance-related offerings, including lead collection and matching as well as the delivery of rate quotes across multiple lines of business. The platform collects consumer requests for insurance quotes online and routes them to pre-qualified agents, brokers, and agencies. In parallel, it presents users with quotes across auto, home, pet, life, final expense, and commercial insurance, positioning itself alongside established comparison and referral platforms.

item.title: PolicyMe partners with Wave
item.description: PolicyMe, a Canadian digital life insurance broker, has partnered with invoicing platform Wave to give small business owners access to life insurance and critical illness coverage. Through Wave Perks, small business owners can now access PolicyMe’s life and critical illness insurance products with a fully digital application process that takes “just minutes” to apply. By partnering with PolicyMe, we’re giving entrepreneurs access to insurance coverage that protects what they’ve worked so hard to build.” – Adnan Glavas, Director of Strategic Partnerships at Wave.

item.title: Encova Goes Live on Duck Creek Distribution Management Platform
item.description: Encova Insurance (Columbus, Ohio) has gone live on an upgraded version of Duck Creek Distribution Management from Duck Creek Technologies (Boston). “Since deploying the latest Duck Creek Distribution Management version, we’ve been able to improve how we support our agency and broker partners,” says Brandon Marshall, SVP, Agency Operations and Marketing, Encova Insurance. “Managing distribution relationships in today’s market demands speed, transparency and adaptability,” says Chris McCloskey, COO, Duck Creek Technologies.
